ocr.text: The evaluation of user satisfaction across various search systems is critical for understanding their effectiveness in providing
accurate information. In this context, the data presented in the table highlights the comparative performance of three distinct
platforms: BioSearch, VFCT, and Sparklis. The reported metrics, particularly the average ratings for the statement "This system
provided me with accurate information," reveal significant differences in user perceptions. BioSearch received the highest
average rating of 4.50, indicating a strong consensus among users regarding its reliability. In contrast, both VFCT and Sparklis
garnered lower ratings of 3.60 and 3.20, respectively, suggesting potential areas for improvement in their information accuracy.
Additionally, the statistical analysis included in the table employs a P-value of 16.183, which underscores the relevance of these
findings.
Questions  Scores: Mean (SD)  F(2, 87)  LSD post-hoc
BioSearch  VFCT  Sparklis  (P-value)  (P < 0.01)
Q1. This system provided me with accurate information.  4.50  3.60  3.20  16.183  BioSearch > VFCT, Sparklis
Q2. This system provided me with abundant information.  3.73  3.63  2.87  6.799  BioSearch, VFCT > Sparklis
Q3. This system helped me easily find information of interest.  3.87  2.47  2.27  24.55  BioSearch > VFCT, Sparklis
Q4. This system helped me clearly understand related information.  3.73  2.70  2.47  13.851  BioSearch > Sparklis, VFCT
Q5. This system rapidly responded to my queries.  3.70  3.90  2.53  14.441  VFCT, BioSearch > Sparklis
Notably, the threshold of P < 0.01 indicates a statistically significant difference in user satisfaction levels among the three
platforms. This information is essential for stakeholders aiming to enhance user experience and optimize search functionalities
in their respective systems. Overall, these insights provide a foundation for ongoing evaluation and development of search
technologies to meet user demands effectively.
